evidence_quotes.quote: Constant surveillance from companies has led to targeted advertising, usually aimed to take my money instead of helping me in need.
Other individuals suffering from a gambling are targeted by publishers using loot boxes in their games to earn money off them, which is really horrible.

paragraphs.text: The Digital Fairness act is a great law that defends consumer rights. Companies have been exploiting consumers for decades, stealing our freedom and causing unnecessary stress. I've been a victim of these practices. Constant surveillance from companies has led to targeted advertising, usually aimed to take my money instead of helping me in need. Other individuals suffering from a gambling are targeted by publishers using loot boxes in their games to earn money off them, which is really horrible. Passing this law could increase the wellbeing of citizens of European countries, as well as serving justice to unfairness. While I do find the Digital Fairness Act great, it requires additional laws to make engaging in online practices more positive. I kindly request for a law to be added in support of a citizen's initiative, known as "Stop Killing Games". Publishers have caused a lot of backlash by closing down games people have paid lots of money for and have enjoyed for years (Examples being: "The Crew" and "Little big planet 3".) It's expected for certain media to be removed or shut down due to a lack of support, However, companies get rid of all functionality from their games, turning it into a paperweight. Players can't play the game they've had fun with because there is no possible way to keep the game up. an additional act to receive protection from game publishers removing video games off the web forever would prevent any unfairness in the Gaming Community. I expect the greatest from the Digital Fairness Act. Thank you for paying attention.
